title: Jonas Stern

'''Jonas Stern''' was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was a [[Baron/Legends|baron]], and who served as a [[Senator/Legends|senator]] and [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. While on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission to [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]], Stern was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] by a [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] agent of [[Slui-Koronar]] in an attempt to win favor with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. When Thrawn discovered Koronar's part in the murder, he had the [[Sluissi]] killed and used the situation to gain an advantage in his negotiations with Stern's replacement.

==Biography==
===Mission to Sluis Van===
Following the fall of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="SQ" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] Jonas Stern rose to become a [[Senator/Legends|senator]] in the fledgling [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="SQ" /> In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],<ref name="6ABY" /> the New Republic embarked on a series of [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] missions to encourage [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] to join its cause. Stern, by this point in his seventies, led one such mission to [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]] as a special envoy of New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]].<ref name="SQ" />

The [[Sluis Van Shipyards]] were historically a major supplier of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] for the Empire, but concerns within the [[Sluissi]] population about the Empire had led the [[Council (Sluis Van)|Sluissi Council]] to remain neutral in the ongoing [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Stern's delegation, which included the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]] and a number of aides, was well received and given access to a large house in an affluent area for the duration of their stay.<ref name="SQ" />

However, the situation was complicated by the arrival of an Imperial delegation led by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], temporarily returning from an extended mission to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Thrawn was well aware that the Sluissi would ultimately side with the New Republic, and intended to retrieve a newly completed [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] from the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], as well as to scout out the [[Sluis system|system]] for future strategic purposes. Thrawn's true intentions were, however, unknown to Stern, to whom the Imperials appeared as rivals for control of the [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="SQ" />

===Murder===
The negotiations were soon underway and, despite some stormy meetings of the Council and some minor incidents between members of the Imperial and Republic delegations, generally proceeded well until the [[Standard day/Legends|night]] the New Republic delegation was due to host a reception for 800 guests at their residence. Stern left much of the preparation work to his aides, who were responsible for organizing catering, [[Music/Legends|music]] and security for the event.<ref name="SQ" />

Several hours before the reception was due to start, Stern met with the former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], working under [[Talon Karrde]] as an [[art dealer]] under the alias Celina Marniss, from whom he bought some valuable artworks, including a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] statuette. The ambassador's collection of sculptures was put on display for the reception, with the Rodian statuette taking pride of place.<ref name="SQ" />

[[File:Thrawn_and_Stern.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Thrawn admires Stern's Rodian statuette.]]
The reception started smoothly, with a cordial atmosphere, despite the presence of dignitaries from both sides of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict, along with the entire Sluissi Council. Stern's art collection, particularly the Rodian statuette, attracted the attention of the visiting Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. Thrawn asked Stern about buying the statuette but the senator was unwilling to part with his new acquisition. Instead, he promised to give Thrawn details on how to contact Celina Marniss. Thrawn did not pursue the matter further and the reception continued late into the night without incident.<ref name="SQ" />

However, the conversation between the Republic and Imperial dignitaries had been overheard by [[Slui-Koronar]] of the pro-Imperial [[United Party]]. The Sluissi saw an opportunity to sabotage the New Republic negotiations and gain favor with the Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]]. Later that night, a [[Defel/Legends|Defel]], hired by Slui-Koronar, infiltrated Stern's residence and [[Death/Legends|murdered]] him in his sleep before stealing the statuette as a gift for Thrawn.<ref name="SQ" />

===Legacy===
Stern's body was found the following morning. When Mon Mothma was informed of the [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador's]] death, she instructed Fey'lya to take over the negotiations until she could dispatch [[Dogh Staya]] to take over from Stern. If anybody inquired as to the baron's whereabouts, they were to be told that he was merely unwell, while the New Republic conducted an investigation.<ref name="SQ" />

News of Stern's death, however, eventually reached Thrawn, who conducted his own investigation. Thrawn uncovered Koronar's involvement in the murder before the New Republic. The Grand Admiral had the Sluissi [[Politics/Legends|politician]] killed, falsifying a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] recording of the latter murder to implicate Republic personnel, and placing 15,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in Stern's account, supposedly from Koronar.<ref name="SQ" />

Upon Staya's arrival, Thrawn used his "evidence" to convince the New Republic to let him take delivery of the Star Destroyer in exchange for withdrawing from the negotiations with the Sluissi. Stern's death was officially announced as the result of a particularly virulent form of [[Flu/Legends|flu]], with all parties involved in the negotiations paying tribute to the baron.<ref name="SQ" />

==Personality and traits==
Stern was a cultured [[Gender/Legends|man]] who valued poetry and sculpture as highly as his diplomatic work. Over time, he acquired a valuable collection of sculptures that became so important to him that he never traveled without them. Though he appeared outwardly congenial, his short temper soon became apparent if his instructions were not followed to the letter.<ref name="SQ" />

==Equipment==
During the reception on Sluis Van, Stern wore a [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]], [[Clothing/Legends|pants]], [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a [[Cape/Legends|cape]].<ref name="SQ" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jonas Stern was created by [[Philippe Rat]] and has only appeared in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] scenario "[[« Statue » quo à Sluis Van]]," in [[Casus Belli 89|issue 89]] of the French gaming magazine [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']], dated [[October]]-[[November]] of [[1995]]. ''Casus Belli'' 89 featured a single illustration of the character by artist [[Rolland Barthélémy]]. As the scenario was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="SQ" /> it is considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>

The article in which Stern appears is set two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the Battle of Endor,<ref name="SQ" /> placing his death in 6 ABY.<ref name="6ABY" /> This is said to be during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Trioculus]],<ref name="SQ" /> which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places a year earlier, in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" /> The scenario also depicts Mara Jade working with Talon Karrde,<ref name="SQ" /> but their first meeting in the short story ''[[First Contact]]'' was in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Fey'lya's role as one of Stern's delegation is at odds with his position as an influential member of the [[New Republic Provisional Council]], as established in [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''The Thrawn Trilogy'']].<ref name="SQ" /><ref name="HttE">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>
